tasks.title: Remove Duplicate Letters (as a foundational string problem)

schedule.tips: Start by thoroughly understanding and implementing solutions to fundamental string manipulation problems.
Leverage hash maps to efficiently solve problems involving lookups and frequency counting.
Be prepared to discuss the trade-offs between different sorting algorithms like merge sort and quick sort.

tasks.title: Zigzag Iterator (can be adapted for tree level order)

schedule.tips: Focus on tree traversals (pre-order, in-order, post-order, level-order) and their applications.
Consider how trees can represent complex relationships and explore graph-like problems that use tree concepts.

tasks.title: Sentence Similarity (can be adapted for linked list manipulation)
Logger Rate Limiter (can be implemented with linked lists/queues)

schedule.tips: Practice manipulating linked lists, including reversing, merging, and detecting cycles.
Master Depth-First Search (DFS) and Breadth-First Search (BFS) for graph traversal problems.

tasks.title: Most Stones Removed with Same Row or Column

schedule.tips: Apply graph algorithms to problems involving connectivity, reachability, and optimization.
Begin with simpler DP problems to grasp the concepts of overlapping subproblems and optimal substructure.

tasks.title: Longest Substring with At Most Two Distinct Characters
Longest Substring with At Most K Distinct Characters

schedule.tips: Practice DP problems that involve optimization and counting, often combining with other data structures.
Understand how to formulate recursive solutions and optimize them with backtracking techniques.

tasks.title: Minimum Domino Rotations For Equal Row
Minimize Max Distance to Gas Station
Minimum Cost to Hire K Workers

schedule.tips: Focus on identifying problems where making the locally optimal choice leads to a globally optimal solution.
Practice problems that require careful handling of edge cases and specific problem constraints.

tasks.title: Review System Design Fundamentals (Scalability, Availability, Latency, CAP Theorem)
How would you split a search query across multiple machines?

schedule.tips: Familiarize yourself with the fundamental principles of designing scalable and robust systems.

tasks.title: Design the game Snake (focus on data structures and core logic)

schedule.tips: Apply your DSA knowledge to think about the data structures and algorithms needed for system components.

tasks.title: Questions from Project: How it works, Bigram and ngram model approach, etc.
Questions from Project: Bi-directional Sync b/w mysql and sqilte db.

schedule.tips: Be ready to articulate the technical details and design choices of your past projects in depth.

tasks.title: Why should one use merge sort over quick sort and vice-versa.
What data structures will you use to develop the game Snake?

schedule.tips: Demonstrate a collaborative mindset and a passion for problem-solving, even in behavioral questions.

tasks.title: Practice solving 2 medium DSA problems under timed conditions.
Simulate a Google phone interview with a focus on explaining thought process.

schedule.tips: Focus on clear communication and thinking aloud during your mock interviews to simulate the real environment.

schedule.focus: Mock Interview 2 (Mixed DSA & System Design Concepts)

tasks.title: Practice 1 medium DSA problem and discuss a simplified system design scenario.
Practice coding on a shared document, emphasizing clear variable names and logic.

schedule.tips: Emphasize clarity in your shared document coding and be ready to pivot between technical problem-solving and system design discussions.

tasks.title: Review your weakest DSA topics and any challenging problems encountered.
Revisit key behavioral scenarios and prepare STAR method responses.

schedule.tips: Consolidate your learning by revisiting areas where you feel less confident, ensuring a strong foundational understanding.

tasks.title: Light review of core concepts and algorithms.
Focus on getting adequate rest and maintaining a positive mindset.

schedule.tips: Approach the interview with confidence, knowing that your preparation has equipped you to showcase your problem-solving abilities.
